pi_items.source: Calculated using impedance formula Z = √(R² + (XL - XC)²) with R=120Ω, L=0.3H, C=33×10⁻⁶F, f=60Hz. XL = 2πfL = 113.097Ω, XC = 1/(2πfC) = 80.381Ω, Z = √(120² + 32.716²) = 124.3798Ω
Specified in task.json metadata and used in verifier.py line 32. Standard engineering tolerance.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and task.json description. File is created and checked by verifier.
Specified in task.json and task description. Used in impedance calculation. Value is consistent with electrical circuit design.
Specified in task.json metadata and task description. Used in impedance calculation with conversion to SI units.
Specified in task.json metadata and task description. Used in impedance calculation with conversion to SI units.
Specified in task.json metadata and task description. Standard AC frequency in North America.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires calculating the total impedance of a series RLC circuit using the Electrical Engineering Calculations app (com.hsn.electricalcalculations, a real app available on Google Play). The ground truth impedance is 124.38 Ohms, calculated from circuit parameters: R=120Ω, L=300mH, C=33µF at 60Hz frequency, using the formula Z=√(R² + (XL-XC)²). The tolerance for acceptance is ±5.0%. The result must be written to /sdcard/tasks/series_rlc_impedance/result.txt as a numeric value. All metadata claims have been independently verified through mathematical calculation and parameter cross-reference.
